2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch adds a new helper function called memory_block_aligned_range() to the Linux kernel's mm/memory module. The function takes a physical address range as input and returns an aligned range, where the start address is rounded up and the end address is rounded down to memory block boundaries. This is necessary for memory hotplug operations, which require ranges to be aligned to memory blocks. The patch also updates the dax/kmem driver to use this new helper function.
